content.text: Ghusl is the full ritual bath required after major impurities such as sexual intercourse, ejaculation, completion of menstruation or postpartum bleeding.

items: At the end of menstruation (haidh) or postpartum bleeding (nifas).
Intend (niyya) to purify oneself with ghusl.
Wash the hands and private parts, remove major impurities.
Pour water over the entire body ensuring wetting the hair and skin; start from head then right side, then left.

content.text: Ensure water reaches all parts of the body. If water is unavailable, tayammum is permitted as concession.
Ghusl returns the believer to a state fit for formal worship — a renewal of body and spirit.
